channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Wildfires burn out of control across Los Angeles area and kill 2 as thousands flee homes
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Massive wildfires roaring through the Los Angeles area left neighborhoods in ruins Wednesday, killing at least two people and threatening landmarks made famous by Hollywood as desperate residents escaped through flames that kept growing with help from hurricane-force winds.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER, CHRISTOPHER WEBER and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about wildfires raining embers onto the Los Angeles area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Two people were killed and more than 1,000 structures were destroyed as fierce wildfires raged in the Los Angeles area. Fast-moving flames burned through homes and businesses as residents fled smoke-filled canyons and picturesque neighborhoods that are home to many celebrities.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US will keep protecting more than 2,000 Rocky Mountain grizzly bears
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — The Biden administration will continue protecting about 2,000 grizzly bears in four Rocky Mountain states despite objections from Republican-led states, U.S. wildlife officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: CNN defamation trial comes at a rough time for legacy media
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — At a particularly inopportune time for legacy media and CNN, the news outlet is on trial in Florida this week, accused of defaming a Navy veteran involved in rescuing endangered Afghans from that country when the U.S. ended its involvement there in 2021.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden is a great-grandfather
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Joe Biden added a new title on Wednesday: great-grandfather. His granddaughter, Naomi Biden Neal, gave birth to her first child, a boy, with husband Peter Neal at Cedars-Sinai Medical Center in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jimmy Carter's life intersected with slavery's legacy. His record on Civil Rights is complicated
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Young Jimmy Carter and his friends were walking across a pasture after a day’s farm labor during the Great Depression. As they came to a gate, his companions stood aside and let Carter enter first. This was no act of kindness or intuitive deference to a future U.S. president.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: TikToker whose speeding BMW killed 6 farmworkers avoids possible 55-year prison sentence with plea deal
item.description: Noah Galle reached a plea deal with prosecutors in which he was sentenced to 12 and a half years in prison after he pleaded guilty to six counts of vehicular homicide for a car crash that left six people dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arkansas woman says box containing stillborn son's belongings stolen
item.description: "That had my stillborn son’s belongings," Watson said, "It had the outfit from when he was in the hospital. And it had his footprint.
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Thornberry, NewsNation

item.title: Wildfires latest: 2 people killed and over 1,000 structures destroyed, per LA fire chief
item.description: Fierce wildfires are raging in the Los Angeles area, fueled by powerful Santa Ana winds, sending residents fleeing from burning homes through flames, ferocious winds and towering clouds of smoke.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Harris's California neighborhood ordered to evacuate as wildfires blaze through area
item.description: Vice President Harris's neighborhood in Los Angeles was ordered to evacuate Tuesday night as wildfires blazed through the area, her spokesperson said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Evacuate, leave picture-taking to the professionals, LA councilmember says
item.description: "This is one of the mightiest sets of fires that we've seen," the president of the Los Angeles City Council said.
item.dc:creator: Nick Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app. Lemon8 resembles an amalgamation of the types of short-form videos found on TikTok and the picture-perfect aesthetic of Instagram and Pinterest.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden says he could've beaten Trump in November
item.description: President Joe Biden said early Wednesday that he thinks he would have beaten President-elect Donald Trump if he stuck with his reelection bid in an interview with USA Today.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: These are the hottest housing markets in 2025: Zillow
item.description: Buffalo, New York is expected to be the hottest major housing market in the U.S., for the second year in a row, according to Zillow.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: California governor says Pacific Palisades wildfire has destroyed many structures as winds kick up
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A wildfire whipped up by extreme winds swept through a Los Angeles hillside dotted with celebrity residences Tuesday, burning homes in Pacific Palisades and prompting evacuation orders for tens of thousands.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JANIE HAR and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The polar vortex brings its bitter cold to the Southern US
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — The plunging polar vortex brought subfreezing temperatures Tuesday to some of the southernmost points of the U.S., threatening to dump snow on parts of Texas and Oklahoma in coming days and contributing to a power outage in Virginia's capital that made the water unsafe to drink.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cybertruck driver used ChatGPT to plan Las Vegas attack, police say
item.description: Police found a 6-page manifesto on Matthew Livelsberger’s phone and say he used ChatGPT to plan his New Year's Day bombing at the front of the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas, the LVMPD said at a news conference Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man who exploded Tesla Cybertruck outside Trump hotel in Las Vegas used generative AI, police say
item.description: The highly decorated soldier who exploded a Tesla Cybertruck outside the Trump hotel in Las Vegas used generative AI including ChatGPT to help plan the attack, Las Vegas police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Migrant caravan being held at resort by Mexican authorities
item.description: Mexican government officials are breaking up migrant caravans headed for the U.S. border and sending them elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Sole survivor of West Valley murder-suicide expected to be released from hospital soon
item.description: The sole survivor of a murder-suicide in West Valley City that left five family members dead is expected to be released from the hospital soon.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Pacific Palisades inferno forces thousands to flee California homes; Gov. Newsom declares state of emergency
item.description: A brush fire that broke out in Southern California Tuesday afternoon has forced officials to order the evacuation of about 30,000 people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palisades Fire in Los Angeles scorches 2,921 acres; many homes destroyed
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - Tens of thousands of residents in the western Los Angeles area were under mandatory evacuation orders Tuesday evening as a wind-driven wildfire exploded in size and swept through communities in the Pacific Palisades. The Palisades Fire was first reported around 10:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: New labels to help identify devices less at risk of hacking
item.description: The federal government is rolling out a consumer labeling system designed to help Americans pick smart devices that are less vulnerable to hacking.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Girl Scouts discontinuing 2 cookie varieties after 2025 season
item.description: The Girl Scouts of the USA have announced plans to discontinue two of their “beloved” cookie offerings after the current cookie season, which kicked off Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Trump delivers first remarks as certified election winner
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump gave remarks on Tuesday morning focused on his economic agenda from his Mar-a-Lago resort in Florida
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, KRON4

item.title: Donald Trump Jr. arrives in Greenland after his father said the U.S. should own the Arctic territory
item.description: The eldest son of President-elect Donald Trump arrived in Greenland on Tuesday for a private visit that heightened speculation that the incoming U.S. administration could seek to take control of the mineral-rich Danish territory that's home to a large U.S. military base.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN and VANESSA GERA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tibet earthquake kills at least 126 people and leaves many trapped
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A strong earthquake shook a high-altitude region of western China and areas of Nepal on Tuesday, damaging hundreds of houses, littering streets with rubble and killing at least 126 people in Tibet. Many others were trapped as dozens of aftershocks shook the remote region.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Donations received to fly migrant girl who lost legs on Mexican train and family
item.description: Inside the Humanitarian Respite Center on Monday a family of a little girl who lost her legs on a train coming to the border were preparing to leave the border after an outpour of donations were sent to help the family.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Getty Images and Shutterstock to merge to become $3.7 billion visual content company
item.description: Visual content companies Shutterstock and Getty Images will join to become a $3.7 billion visual content company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 found dead in compartment of JetBlue plane at Florida airport
item.description: The two were found dead during a routine post-flight maintenance inspection on Monday, according to JetBlue.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington for three days of state funeral rites starting Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Strong earthquake kills at least 95 people in western China near Mount Everest
item.description: A strong earthquake shook a high-altitude region of western China and areas of Nepal on Tuesday, damaging hundreds of houses, littering streets with rubble, and killing at least 95 people in Tibet. Many others were trapped as dozens of aftershocks shook the remote region.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt in Georgia election workers case
item.description: A federal judge held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt on Monday for failing to comply with court orders in two former Georgia election workers’ efforts to collect their $146 million defamation judgment. At the end of a two-day hearing that saw Giuliani take the stand, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Flight cancellations pile up as major winter storm hits East Coast
item.description: Flight cancellations and delays are stacking up as a major winter storm makes its way across the East Coast.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Congress certifies Trump's election win despite weather threat
item.description: Congress certified President-elect Donald Trump’s election win on Monday as Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) plowed ahead with the vote despite a snowstorm set to hit Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: Border agents in California find child allegedly abducted from Texas
item.description: U.S. Customs and Border Protection said agents recovered a missing child last week and apprehended a driver wanted for their alleged abduction.
item.dc:creator: TMX, KTLA 5

item.title: Why you shouldn't charge your phone to 100%, according to experts
item.description: It may be tempting to charge to 100%, but experts say 80% may be enough — and beneficial for your phone. Here's why.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4
